consistency.data_source: The data appears consistent with standard D&D 5th Edition Rogue class features, specifically the 'Expertise' feature.
consistency.terminology: Terminology ('skill proficiencies', 'thieves' tools', 'proficiency bonus') is standard and used correctly.
consistency.structure: The structure clearly defines the feature name, class, level of acquisition, and tiered benefits.

quality.completeness: The description covers both the initial acquisition at level 1 and the enhancement at level 6.
quality.clarity: The description is clear. Doubling the proficiency bonus is a distinct and powerful mechanic.
quality.readability: High readability; mechanics are straightforward to understand.

mechanics.level_1_effect: Doubling proficiency bonus (effectively granting Expertise) for two chosen skills or one skill and thieves' tools.
mechanics.level_6_effect: Adds two more selections for Expertise.
mechanics.balance_assessment: This is a core Rogue feature, mechanically sound, and balanced for its level of power/frequency of use.

zod_validation.conclusion: No ZOD errors were reported, suggesting the data structure is sound according to whatever schema ZOD uses.

feature_summary.name: Expertise
feature_summary.key_mechanic: Doubles proficiency bonus for selected skills/tools (Level 1: 2 choices; Level 6: 2 more choices).
